text.title: Video Structure and Optimization
text.summary: Hook-first video structure, title and thumbnail rules, and description template.

rule.statement: Write the hook before the rest of the script. The first 30 seconds determine whether viewers stay; a strong hook doubles effective watch time.
rule.rationale: YouTube's algorithm weights watch time and session time. A video with a poor hook that loses viewers in the first 30 seconds generates negative distribution signals regardless of total content quality.

list.item: 0:00-0:30 — Hook: grab attention, preview value (one of: question, promise, curiosity, contrarian)
0:30-1:00 — Intro: who you are, credibility signal, agenda
1:00-near end — Content: value delivery with chapter markers
Near end — CTA: subscribe, watch next video, product link (30 sec max)

example.note: Question hook: "Ever wondered why your YouTube videos get views but zero subscribers?" — creates tension, promises answer, audience self-selects.

rule.statement: Structure every title as: [Primary keyword] + [Benefit or outcome] + [Optional curiosity element]. Keep under 60 characters.
rule.rationale: Title and thumbnail must be designed together as a single unit — the combination drives CTR. Keyword in the title signals search relevance; benefit + curiosity drives the click.

example.note: "How to Get Your First 1000 Subscribers (What Actually Works)" — keyword first, outcome stated, curiosity parenthetical.
"My YouTube Journey Update #47" — no keyword, no benefit, no audience signal. Only works for established channels with loyal subscribers.

rule.statement: Every thumbnail must be readable at 120x68 pixels (mobile thumbnail size) before publishing.
rule.rationale: Most YouTube impressions appear at small thumbnail sizes on mobile. A thumbnail that only reads at large size is effectively invisible to the majority of the audience.

list.item: Face with expression (surprise, excitement) — highest CTR signal
Contrasting colors against YouTube's white/dark background
Consistent style and branding across all thumbnails

section.title: Description and Chapters

rule.statement: Add timestamp chapters to all videos above 8 minutes. YouTube surfaces individual chapters in search results, increasing impressions per video.
rule.rationale: Chapter timestamps generate additional SERP appearances for each chapter heading, multiplying the indexable surface area of a single video.

section.reference: YouTube description template with keyword hook, timestamps, resources, tags
